title: HD Hyundai Oilbank Signs Offtake Agreement with Korean Air
content: HD Hyundai Oilbank has entered into an offtake agreement with Korean Air to supply sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) for the Incheonâ€“Kobe route. The deal begins in September 2025 and lasts through the end of 2026. Under the agreement, Hyundai Oilbank will be the exclusive SAF supplier for about 90 flights. This arrangement is Hyundai Oilbankâ€™s first major push into supplying SAF domestically. Previously, the company had exported SAF to airlines in Japan (e.g. All Nippon Airways). The timing is significant, as South Korea gears up for a mandatory SAF blending regulation that will come into force in 2027. The agreement is also a strategic move to build up commercial supply infrastructure ahead of more stringent future mandates. South Koreaâ€™s government plans to require a 1% SAF blend for all international flights starting in 2027, with proposals to increase that to 3-5% by 2030 and 7-10% by 2035. For Korean Air, the deal supports its efforts to expand the use of domestically produced SAF on international routes, reducing reliance on imported fuels and aligning with global aviation decarbonization goals. It also positions both companies as early movers in the Korean SAF market, which is expected to scale rapidly once regulatory blending targets take effect.
author: Analysts Account
